docs.title: Welcome to Here & There Michigan
docs.text:
Here & There is centered around genuine connection through shared real world experiences. Its primary purpose is to create friendships, strengthen social circles, and encourage meaningful connections by bringing people together through in person events and activities.

While conversation and interaction within the group are welcome, the heart of the group is participation; showing up, meeting others, and building the kind of connection that develops more naturally face to face.

Members are encouraged to contribute in ways that support a thoughtful, respectful, and enjoyable atmosphere. The group is intended for people who value authentic interaction, social ease, and a positive environment where friendships and community can grow over time.

Events may range from simple gatherings to planned outings, but the shared goal remains the same: creating opportunities to connect beyond the screen and make real world social life more accessible, comfortable, and rewarding.

docs.title: Not a Member?
docs.text:
Ready to meet new people and make real connections? Join us and check out what’s coming up. Already a member? Be sure to browse our upcoming events and jump in on something that interests you.

Learn How To Join

docs.title: Already a Member?
docs.text:
Be sure to browse our upcoming events and jump in on something that interests you.

View Upcoming Events

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text: Here & There is a community built on respect, positivity, and genuine connection. We encourage thoughtful conversation, supportive interaction, and participation that helps everyone feel welcome and included. To get the most out of the group, please take a moment to review and follow the best practices outlined in the sections below.

docs.title: Introduce Yourself
docs.text:
We highly encourage new members to create an introduction post as soon as you feel comfortable doing so. This helps put a friendly face to a name and makes it much easier for others to welcome you and start meaningful conversations. A great introduction goes beyond simply saying hello. Share a bit about yourself, such as your age, where you are from, and what brought you to Here & There. Let people know whether you are interested in dating, making new friends, or simply being part of an active and social community.

Adding a few details about your hobbies, interests, or how you like to spend your free time gives others an easy way to connect with you. Including one or two recent photos of yourself is also encouraged, as it helps build trust and keeps the group feeling personal and welcoming. There is no need to overthink it. Just be yourself, share what you are comfortable with, and enjoy getting to know the community.

docs.title: View & Attend Events
docs.text: Attending events is one of the best ways to get the most out of Here & There. Meeting in person helps turn conversations into real connections and makes it easier to build friendships in a welcoming setting. Events give members a chance to feel included, meet people they might not otherwise interact with, and truly experience the community beyond the Facebook group.

docs.title: Host An Event
docs.text:
Members are always welcome to create and host their own events within Here & There. If you have an idea for a get together, activity, or outing you think others would enjoy, we encourage you to share it with the group. Member created events help keep the community active, diverse, and driven by the interests of the people who are part of it. To learn even more about how to create and host an event, please read this how to guide.

How To Host

docs.title: Questions or concerns?
docs.text: If you ever have a question, concern, or run into something that feels unclear, please feel free to reach out to an admin. We are here to help, provide guidance when needed, and support a positive experience for everyone in the group. Open communication helps the group run smoothly and ensures that issues can be addressed thoughtfully and respectfully.

docs.title: FAQs
docs.text:
For full documentation visit mkdocs.org.

What is Material for MkDocs?

Material for MkDocs is a theme built on top of MkDocs that transforms documentation sites with a modern, responsive design inspired by Google's Material Design principles.

Is Material for MkDocs free?

Yes, the core theme is free and open source. All features, previously exclusive to the Insiders version, are now available to everyone.

docs.title: How to Create an Event in Here & There
1) Create the Event
docs.text:
• Click the Events tab
  • Select Create Event

docs.title: a. Event Name
docs.text:
Keep the title short, clear, and engaging, and always include the city name.

Including the city helps members quickly identify where an event is taking place when browsing the event list on mobile or desktop. Not everyone will recognize a venue name, band name, movie title, or local landmark without location context.

Examples: - Bowling & Fun – Novi - Movies – Lansing - Live Band & Drinks – Flint - Cards & Games – Kalamazoo - Roller Skating – Warren

Important: The city name is required and should appear early enough in the title to be visible in event listings.

docs.title: b. Date and Time
docs.text:
Enter the correct date and time.

If there is a specific entry time or staggered arrival, use the intended arrival time and clarify in the description when the event officially begins.

docs.title: c. Address
docs.text:
Always include the full address.

This allows members to click directly into map navigation.

docs.title: d. Who Can See It
docs.text: This should always be set to Here & There Michigan.

docs.title: e. Group Event Privacy
docs.text: Set privacy to Members Only (Do not make the event public.)

docs.title: a. Be Specific About Location
docs.text:
Remember this is a statewide group, and many members may not know local nicknames for venues, parks, or gathering spots.

For example, saying "Montrose Pub" may not help someone unfamiliar with the area unless the town is also identified.

docs.title: b. Help Members Feel Comfortable Attending
docs.text:
Include details that make the event inviting: - Mention if a table or area is reserved - Let members know how to recognize the group - Post a photo once you arrive - Describe what to look for

If someone offers to serve as a wingman, meet people outside, or help new members walk in, that can make a big difference—especially for newer, shy, or introverted members.

docs.title: c. Include Helpful Logistics
docs.text: If applicable, mention: - Costs - Ticket requirements - Limited seating - Parking details - Reservation needs - Anything useful for someone attending for the first time

docs.title: 4) Add a Co-Host
docs.text:
You may add one co-host after the event is created (desktop is often easiest for this).

If it is your first time hosting, feel free to ask someone experienced to help.

docs.title: a. Edit the Event Photo
docs.text: Click Edit on the top banner image.

docs.title: b. Choose a Photo That Matches the Activity
docs.text: Use an image that visually represents the event.

docs.title: c. Avoid Screenshots
docs.text:
Please avoid screenshots of flyers or other events that contain text, dates, or unrelated details.

Better options include: - A venue photo - A band image - A hiking trail - Bowling lanes - Roller skates - A generic activity image

A strong photo helps your event stand out and makes it easier for members to find later.

docs.title: 6) Create the Event
docs.text: Click Create Event — and you're done 🎉

docs.title: Additional Notes
docs.text:
• Once created, the event automatically appears in the Here & There feed, so there is no need to immediately repost it.
  • Promote your event thoughtfully. Sharing about once per week is usually enough at first, then increase reminders as the date approaches.
  • Add fresh text when reposting rather than simply resharing without context.
  • Encourage conversation directly on the event page—welcoming attendees and responding to comments helps visibility through group algorithms.

docs.title: What You Need as an Event Host
Name Tags
docs.text: Simple sticker name tags and a marker work perfectly.

docs.title: A Visible Marker
docs.text: Having something noticeable such as a balloon, table sign, or distinctive clothing helps members identify the group easily.

docs.title: Why Visibility Matters
docs.text:
A visible, welcoming group naturally attracts attention—especially when everyone is engaged, laughing, and enjoying themselves.

It also helps first-time attendees, shy members, introverts, and those attending alone feel confident that they have found the right group.

docs.title: Important Reminder
docs.text:
Check your event regularly.

Members often ask questions before an event, and timely responses help attendance and improve the overall experience.

docs.title: How To Become A Member
docs.text:
Once you tap the join button on Facebook, you will be presented with a form that asks you a series of questions, and to confirm you have read the group rules. These questions help us maintain a safe, positive community and ensure the group guidelines are met.

  1. Where in Michigan are you located, how old are you, and what is your current relationship status?
  2. What kinds of in person events are you most interested in?
  3. Have you read the group rules? Please include the keyword listed there in your answer.

docs.title: Ready To Join?
docs.text:
If you are ready to join a positive and welcoming community, we invite you to become a member.

Become A Member

docs.title: Group Rules
docs.text:
1. Be kind & respectful, no hate speech or bullying

Treat everyone with kindness and respect. Hate speech, harassment, bullying, or personal attacks of any kind will not be tolerated. We encourage members to focus on positivity, support fellow members, and contribute to a friendly, uplifting atmosphere.

2. Respect personal boundaries

Members are expected to engage in ways that support a comfortable social environment. Do not send unwanted private messages, pressure members for personal information, or continue contact after someone has expressed disinterest. Repeated unwanted attention, private solicitation, recruitment for personal arrangements, or behavior that causes others to feel targeted may result in removal.

3. No spam or self promotion

Advertising, self-promotion, fundraising, recruiting, and repetitive promotion of outside interests are not permitted without admin approval.

4. Posts should encourage meaningful engagement

Content that does not create a clear discussion, offer useful context, or support the purpose of the group, including vague check-ins, low-effort posts, or attention-based content may be moderated. This space exists for social connection, shared experiences, and meaningful participation. Posts should generally align with that purpose.

5. Choose topics with the group atmosphere in mind.

Political debate, explicit sexual content, and other topics likely to create unnecessary tension or shift the atmosphere away from the group’s purpose may be removed

6. Give events room to succeed

Event hosts plan around attendance. If you respond “going,” please make a genuine effort to attend or update your response if plans change. Hosts may set reasonable limits based on venue size, timing, reservations, or event format. Please respect host guidance, venue staff, and the practical needs of each event. Repeated late cancellations, habitual no-shows, arriving without RSVP when space is limited, or behavior that disrupts events may affect continued participation.

7. Respect privacy inside and outside the group

Do not share screenshots, private conversations, personal details, or event photos of others without consideration for their comfort and consent. What is shared here should generally stay within the spirit of the group unless clearly intended otherwise.

8. Authentic profiles and participation matter

Profiles should appear genuine and active. Accounts that appear misleading, incomplete, or inconsistent with group expectations may be declined or removed. When joining, include the word respect in your answer to the rules question as confirmation that you have read through the guidelines. Authentic profiles are expected to include a visible profile photo and signs of recent activity within the previous 12 months. Must be a Michigan resident and 21 years of age or older.

9. Admin decisions are final

Not every situation can be anticipated in writing. Admins may moderate posts, comments, or membership when necessary to maintain the tone and purpose of the group. Moderation decisions are made to support the overall health of the group and may not always be publicly debated. Blocking an admin or mod will result in your removal.
